title: Cynanchum longiflorum
intro: Cynanchum longiflorum, also known as Asiatic foxglove or Long-flower Cynanchum, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, traditionally used in medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Cynanchum longiflorum include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, including cynanchine, longiflorin, and ursolic acid, among others. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into tonics and syrups, to support respiratory health and combat infection.
taxonomy: Cynanchum longiflorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Cynanchum, which is a group of plants commonly known as swallowworts. This plant is native to Asia and is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Cynanchum longiflorum is a perennial or shrub-like plant that produces clusters of white or purple flowers. The exact classification of the plant may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic revisions.

parts: Cynanchum longiflorum, commonly known as the yellow trumpetvine or yellow gentian, is a plant with a long history of use in traditional medicine. The root, stem, and leaves of this plant are used in various medicinal preparations. In traditional Chinese medicine, the roots of Cynanchum longiflorum are valued for their cooling properties and are often used to treat fever, headache, and other heat-related conditions.
